RTP sat this one out — no votes logged from that office this round, so this week is an HQ-only story. We'll pick the office comparison back up next time RTP checks in.

# How decisive were these results?

Hat is the odd one out this week with three options, so it gets its own test; everything else is a straight two-way race.


::: {.cell}

:::


**TL;DR:** Lotion trounced Spray (57–15, 79%) for the most one-sided call of the week. Shades was the real toss-up: Wayfarer edged Aviator 38–34, barely distinguishable from a coin flip.


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}


|question  |  N|Leader (votes)         |Trailer (votes)          |Leader share | Margin (votes)|Margin (points) | p vs 50/50|Leader 95% CI | Votes to flip|
|:---------|--:|:----------------------|:------------------------|:------------|--------------:|:---------------|----------:|:-------------|-------------:|
|Burn Care | 72|Aloe (57)              |Moisturizing Lotion (15) |79.2%        |             42|58.3%           |   7.00e-07|[0.68, 0.88]  |            22|
|Method    | 77|Reapply Sunscreen (48) |Long Sleeves (29)        |62.3%        |             19|24.7%           |   3.95e-02|[0.51, 0.73]  |            10|
|Shades    | 72|Aviator (38)           |Wayfarer (34)            |52.8%        |              4|5.6%            |   7.24e-01|[0.41, 0.65]  |             3|

## The three-way race: Hat


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}


|option                  | votes|share |
|:-----------------------|-----:|:-----|
|Baseball Cap            |    50|63.3% |
|Wide-brimmed Floppy Hat |    18|22.8% |
|Bucket Hat              |    11|13.9% |


:::
:::


Baseball Cap isn't just the leader, it's the clear favorite: 63% of the vote versus Bucket Hat and Wide-brimmed Floppy Hat splitting the rest evenly. A chi-square goodness-of-fit test against "all three hats equally popular" gives p = 7.41\times 10^{-8} — nowhere close to what we'd expect if hat preference were random. This office has a type.

# Do the sun-safety-conscious cluster together?

Reapplying sunscreen and reaching for aloe after a burn both sound like the habits of the same sun-safety-minded person. Let's bound the overlap.


::: {.cell}

:::


Of the 72 overlapping voters, somewhere between 28 and 48 (39%–67%) could be both Reapply-Sunscreen *and* Aloe people, versus about 36 (49%) expected if the two habits were unrelated. Given how popular both options already are on their own, there's a good floor under the "actually sun-safe" coalition — good news for everyone's skin.
